scope_note: QA E8 Satellite Chamber cert [496]. Primary sources: Wildberger, N.J. (2005) Divine Proportions Wild Egg Books ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8 (QA T-operator, BEDA tuples, orbit classification, rational trigonometry quadrance G=d^2+e^2); Bourbaki (1968) Groupes et algebres de Lie Ch. 4-6 (E8 root system, Weyl chambers, Dynkin diagram); Humphreys, J.E. (1972) Introduction to Lie Algebras and Representation Theory Springer ISBN 978-0-387-90053-7 (E8 simple roots, Cartan matrix). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): For QA mod m=9 Satellite orbit (8 states, period 8), canonically anchored at (6,3) (unique (3,4,5)/C<F step), the height function h = alpha*d^2 + beta*e^2 lies in the E8 Weyl chamber where (6,3) is the branch node and (3,6) [Grant LRT] is the long-arm terminal leaf if and only if 7/12 < alpha/beta < 13/12. The isotropic condition alpha=beta selects the unique ray h = c*(d^2+e^2), normalized to h = G = d^2+e^2. Furthermore: (1) G=d^2+e^2 is unique among the 7 elementary QA invariants {b,e,d,a,C,F,G} satisfying strict ordering + genericity + correct branch; (2) G and G^2 have identical Satellite-axis ordering but lie in different E8 Weyl chambers (6 explicit Type 2 wall roots differ in sign); (3) parity reduction b+e+d+a+C+F+G ≡ b (mod 2) holds for all 576 QA pairs; (4) C^2+F^2=G^2 for all (b,e) (QA Pythagorean triple). Theorem NT: orbit steps and root computations are integer arithmetic; float projections are observer outputs only. Lineage: derived 2026-06-21 through iterative algebraic session.
